question: Is this stock cheap on an EV/EBITDA basis relative to its sector?
system_instruction: N/A — mechanical, computed by formula (see formula_or_data_needed)
formula_or_data_needed: EV/EBITDA = Enterprise Value / EBITDA (trailing 12mo). Common industry benchmarks run 6x-12x depending on sector (utilities lower, tech/software higher) — score relative to sector median, not an absolute number. < sector median -> 2, roughly in line -> 1, > sector median -> 0. Source: EV/EBITDA screening methodology, quant-investing.com / footnotesanalyst.com (2026 research pass).

question: Does this stock have a high free cash flow yield (real cash generation priced cheaply)?
system_instruction: N/A — mechanical, computed by formula (see formula_or_data_needed)
formula_or_data_needed: FCF Yield = Free Cash Flow (ttm) / Enterprise Value * 100. Top-decile FCF-yield stocks have historically outperformed the broader market by 3-5%/yr (quant-investing.com backtest). >8% -> 2, 4-8% -> 1, <4% -> 0. Use a 5-year average for cyclical sectors to avoid a single peak/trough year distorting the read.

question: Is the stock trading at or below its Benjamin Graham Number?
system_instruction: N/A — mechanical, computed by formula (see formula_or_data_needed)
formula_or_data_needed: Graham Number = sqrt(22.5 * EPS * Book Value Per Share) — combines a max P/E of 15 and max P/B of 1.5, one of the most conservative classic value formulas (Graham, The Intelligent Investor). Price <= Graham Number -> 2, within 25% above it -> 1, >25% above -> 0. Most reliable for stable, asset-rich businesses; explicitly less reliable for high-growth/tech names with little book value — note that caveat in the score reasoning.

question: Is this stock cheap on a Price/Sales basis relative to its sector?
system_instruction: N/A — mechanical, computed by formula (see formula_or_data_needed)
formula_or_data_needed: P/S = Market Cap / Revenue (ttm). Compare to sector median rather than an absolute cutoff (P/S norms vary hugely by sector — software commands higher multiples than retail/industrials on revenue alone). < sector median -> 2, near median -> 1, > sector median -> 0.

question: Is this stock cheap on a Price/Book basis?
system_instruction: N/A — mechanical, computed by formula (see formula_or_data_needed)
formula_or_data_needed: P/B = Market Cap / Book Value. Classic Graham/deep-value threshold: P/B < 1.5 -> 2, 1.5-3.0 -> 1, >3.0 -> 0. Least meaningful for asset-light/high-IP businesses (software, services) where book value understates true asset base — flag that caveat when scoring such a name.

question: Is the stock's forward P/E meaningfully cheaper than its own trailing P/E or historical average?
system_instruction: N/A — mechanical, computed by formula (see formula_or_data_needed)
formula_or_data_needed: Compare forward P/E (next-12mo consensus estimate) to trailing P/E and to the stock's own 5-year average P/E. Forward P/E >15% below both -> 2 (market pricing in real improvement cheaply), forward roughly in line -> 1, forward P/E higher than trailing (deteriorating outlook priced in) -> 0.

question: Is the EV/Sales multiple reasonable given the company's actual margin profile?
system_instruction: N/A — mechanical, computed by formula (see formula_or_data_needed)
formula_or_data_needed: EV/Sales should scale with profitability — a business with high EV/Sales needs high margins to justify it. Compute EV/Sales / (Operating Margin * 100); lower is better (cheaper multiple per unit of margin quality). Below sector-median ratio -> 2, in line -> 1, above -> 0.

question: Is management returning meaningful capital to shareholders relative to the stock's price?
system_instruction: N/A — mechanical, computed by formula (see formula_or_data_needed)
formula_or_data_needed: Shareholder Yield = Dividend Yield + Net Buyback Yield (change in shares outstanding, annualized). >5% combined -> 2, 2-5% -> 1, <2% or net share issuance -> 0. Captures total capital return, not just dividends — a common institutional value-screen addition.

question: Is the stock cheap on a Price/Free-Cash-Flow basis?
system_instruction: N/A — mechanical, computed by formula (see formula_or_data_needed)
formula_or_data_needed: Price/FCF = Market Cap / Free Cash Flow (ttm). AAII's own value screen threshold: < 15 -> 2 (cheap cash generator), 15-25 -> 1, >25 -> 0. Distinct from EV/FCF-yield (f2) in using equity value not enterprise value — flags cases where a heavy debt load makes EV/EBITDA look worse than the equity-only multiple suggests, or vice versa.

question: Is the stock's current valuation multiple well below its own historical norm (a mean-reversion setup)?
system_instruction: N/A — mechanical, computed by formula (see formula_or_data_needed)
formula_or_data_needed: Compare current P/E (or EV/EBITDA if P/E is negative/not meaningful) to the stock's own trailing 5-year average multiple. Current multiple <70% of the 5yr average -> 2 (real de-rating, worth asking why), 70-100% -> 1, at/above its own historical average -> 0. Requires multi-year fundamentals history — flag N/A if fewer than 3 years of data available rather than computing off a short window.

question: Has revenue grown at a strong, sustained rate over the last 3 years?
system_instruction: N/A — mechanical, computed by formula (see formula_or_data_needed)
formula_or_data_needed: 3-year Revenue CAGR = (Revenue_now / Revenue_3yr_ago)^(1/3) - 1. >15%/yr -> 2, 5-15%/yr -> 1, <5%/yr -> 0. Use CAGR over a full 3-year window, not a single strong/weak year, to avoid rewarding one lumpy quarter.

question: Has EPS grown at a strong, sustained rate over the last 3 years?
system_instruction: N/A — mechanical, computed by formula (see formula_or_data_needed)
formula_or_data_needed: 3-year EPS CAGR, same method as g1 applied to diluted EPS. >15%/yr -> 2, 5-15%/yr -> 1, <5%/yr or negative -> 0. Watch for buyback-driven EPS growth masking flat/declining net income — cross-check against revenue growth (g1); a large gap between EPS growth and revenue growth is a flag worth noting in the reasoning even though it doesn't change this score.

question: Is revenue growth itself accelerating, not just positive?
system_instruction: N/A — mechanical, computed by formula (see formula_or_data_needed)
formula_or_data_needed: Compare YoY revenue growth rate in the most recent quarter to the prior 1-2 quarters' YoY growth rates. Accelerating for 2+ consecutive quarters -> 2 (signals strengthening demand, share gains, or a successful product launch per estimate-revision/acceleration research), flat -> 1, decelerating -> 0. This is a trend-of-a-trend metric — genuinely different information from g1's static 3yr average.

question: Are Wall Street analysts revising their earnings estimates UP for this stock?
system_instruction: N/A — mechanical, computed by formula (see formula_or_data_needed)
formula_or_data_needed: Consensus forward-EPS estimate today vs. the same consensus estimate 90 days ago. Revised up >5% -> 2, roughly flat -> 1, revised down -> 0. Grounded in the earnings-forecast-revision factor literature (Guerard et al., composite CTEF model) — one of the more persistently documented return factors in academic finance, not just a momentum heuristic. Needs historical consensus-estimate data, which may not be available from every data source — leave N/A honestly if no estimate-revision history is fetchable rather than guessing.

question: Does this company clear the 'Rule of 40' (growth + profitability together)?
system_instruction: N/A — mechanical, computed by formula (see formula_or_data_needed)
formula_or_data_needed: Rule of 40 score = Revenue growth % (yoy) + FCF margin % (FCF / Revenue). >=40 -> 2, 30-40 -> 1, <30 -> 0. Common SaaS/growth-software quality heuristic — a fast-grower with weak margins or a profitable business with weak growth can both still clear it; only genuinely weak on both dimensions fails. Most relevant for growth/compounder archetypes, less meaningful for deep-value turnarounds where growth may be intentionally negative during a restructuring.

question: Is operating margin actually expanding year-over-year, not just positive?
system_instruction: N/A — mechanical, computed by formula (see formula_or_data_needed)
formula_or_data_needed: Current-quarter (or ttm) operating margin vs. the same period one year ago. Expanded >200bps -> 2 (growing profitably, operating leverage kicking in), roughly flat (+/-200bps) -> 1, contracted -> 0. Distinct from Category B's static operating-margin-positive check (b11) — this is about the *trend*, not the level.
